name: Unified Animation Heartbeat
requirements: TEA update cycle (to consume the pulse)

userStory.iWant: all animations to be driven by a single system clock
userStory.soThat: multiple simultaneous animations (springs, tweens) stay perfectly in sync and don't overwhelm the event loop.

acceptanceCriteria: A single global interval/pulse is managed by the EventBus
Components subscribe to the pulse via a specialized Msg type
All spring/tween physics calculations are batched into one update cycle per frame
The pulse frequency (FPS) is configurable at the runtime level
backwardCompatibility: Legacy components using individual setIntervals will still function, but will be deprecated in favor of the pulse-aware animate() helper.

outScope: Individual component logic (they just consume the pulse)

testPlan.goldenPath: Spawn 50 'wobbly' springs at once. Assert that only one setInterval is active and all 50 values update in the same event loop turn.
edgeCases: Slow systems where the pulse can't maintain the target FPS
Pausing/Resuming the heartbeat (e.g. when terminal is minimized)
testPlan.fuzzStress: Run 500 animations at 120fps for 10 minutes and measure CPU spikes and frame drop counts.
failureCases: Pulse feedback loop (update cycle triggering another pulse instantly)

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(N) where N is active animations
